The API requires the user to have Create Projects permissions.

Opens and schedules a new ClangTidy analysis session, returning the session key.

schema.description: The project ID.

This parameter can be used to pass a timestamp giving the time (in milliseconds since 1970) for which the data should be provided or "HEAD" to refer to the most recent commit. This can optionally be prefixed by the name of the branch, followed by a colon (by default, we use the default branch). You can append a "p1" to resolve to the immediate parent of the most recent commit at the given branch/timestamp. p2 would resolve to the parent of the immediate parent and so on. In merge commits, the parent resolution considers only the first parent. You can append "e" to include the potential external report uploads of the commit. A parameter value that uses all features would be "master:1601637680000p1e"

Triggers the creation of a backup and returns its ID.
This service is public API since Teamscale 2024.8.
The user needs to have the permission to export global data provided the backup contains any and the permission to backup project data for all projects contained in the backup.

backup-global.description: Include global data in the backup.

backup-path.description: The backup path. If this is not set, a new internal path will be generated.

export-option-branch.description: The branch (pattern) for which rules are applied. May be present multiple times.

items.description: The branch (pattern) for which rules are applied. May be present multiple times.

export-option-date.description: The start date for export of external data. May be present multiple times.

items.description: The start date for export of external data. May be present multiple times.

export-option-granularity.description: The granularity to apply for export of external data (DISCARD, DAILY, WEEKLY, PRESERVE). May be present multiple times.

export-option-partition.description: The partition (pattern) for which rules are applied. May be present multiple times.

items.description: The partition (pattern) for which rules are applied. May be present multiple times.

export-option-project.description: The partition (pattern) for which rules are applied. May be present multiple times.

items.description: The partition (pattern) for which rules are applied. May be present multiple times.

export-option-target.description: The target to apply for export of external data. May be present multiple times.

include-all-visible-projects.description: Includes all user visible projects in the backup.

include-project.description: Include project data in the backup. May be present multiple times.

400.description: include-projects param has to be empty if include-all-visible-projects is set to true

401.description: No authentication header was provided or the credentials were invalid.

post.summary: Handles the incoming requests from the Bitbucket Cloud web hooks.
post.description:
Handles the incoming requests from the Bitbucket cloud web hooks and processes the request body depending on the event. The events which are handled by the service are 'repo:push', 'pullrequest:created', 'pullrequest:updated' and 'pullrequest:fulfilled'.
The API requires no login

post.summary: Handles the incoming requests from the Bitbucket on-premise server.
post.description:
Handles the incoming requests from the Bitbucket on-premise server and processes the request body depending on the event. The events which are handled by the service are 'repo:refs_changed', 'pr:opened', 'pr:modified', 'pr:merged', 'pr:declined' and 'pr:deleted'.
The API requires no login

get.description:
Returns, for the given uniform path, a map of metric name to metric value (Double or Assessment), depending on the type of metric.
Requires 'View Project' permission for all given projects

schema.description: This parameter can be used to pass a timestamp giving the time (in milliseconds since 1970) for which the data should be provided or "HEAD" to refer to the most recent commit. This can optionally be prefixed by the name of the branch, followed by a colon (by default, we use the default branch). You can append a "p1" to resolve to the immediate parent of the most recent commit at the given branch/timestamp. p2 would resolve to the parent of the immediate parent and so on. In merge commits, the parent resolution considers only the first parent. You can append "e" to include the potential external report uploads of the commit. A parameter value that uses all features would be "master:1601637680000p1e"

parameters.name: absolute-metric
parameters.description: A list of metric names that will be evaluated and returned. If not set, ALL metrics of the schema are used.

parameters.name: density
parameters.description: A list of density metric names that will be evaluated and returned (e.g. Branch Coverage, or Finding Density Red).

parameters.name: assessment
parameters.description: A list of assessment metric names that will be evaluated and returned (e.g. Cyclomatic Complexity Assessment).

parameters.name: project
parameters.description: A list of metric names that will be evaluated and returned. If not set, ALL metrics of the schema are used.

post.summary: Create or update external finding descriptors from a Parasoft report.
post.description:
Given a Parasoft C/C++test findings report in the payload, this service creates or updates external finding descriptors, updates the specified analysis profile by adding the rules from the report to the analysis profile. In addition, the finding schema of all projects referencing the profile are updated.
The API requires the user to have Edit permissions on the Analysis Profile .

parameters.name: analysis-profile
parameters.description: The name of the analysis profile to be updated. It cannot be null.

report.description: Given a Parasoft C/C++test findings report in the payload, this service creates or updates external finding descriptors, updates the specified analysis profile by adding the rules from the report to the analysis profile. In addition, the finding schema of all projects referencing the profile are updated.

401.description: No authentication header was provided or the credentials were invalid.

403.description: The user is authenticated, but does not have the necessary permissions to access the endpoint.

The API requires the user to have Edit permissions on the Analysis Profile .

parameters.name: analysis-profile

post.description:
Triggers creation of a snapshot of this instance and returns the ID of the created snapshot. Additionally, a remote snapshot with the same parameters can be created and a comparison between the two scheduled.
The API requires the user to have Access Administrative Services permissions.

branch-name.description: The branch name for which to create the instance comparison snapshot.

detailed-snapshot.description: Whether a more detailed snapshot should be created (i.e. includes which findings are present). May slow down the snapshot creation and comparison computation depending on instance size.

end-timestamp.description: The timestamp used to determine when to cut off comparison. Useful when one instance has an ongoing analysis, and the other does not.

project-exclusion-pattern.description: Optional regex pattern to specify which projects should be excluded from the snapshot.

project-inclusion-pattern.description: Optional regex pattern to specify which projects should be included in the snapshot.

remote-access-token.description: Access token for the remote instance. May be empty, in this case access token of locally logged in user is assumed.

remote-user.description: User name for the remote instance. May be empty, in this case locally logged in user is assumed.

schedule-remote-comparison.description: Whether to schedule a comparison with a remote instance that uses the same parameters.

Returns the status (IN_PROGRESS, SUCCESS or FAILURE) of a single status identified by the given ID or ID prefix. Only snapshots created by the logged-in user are considered for this operation.
The API requires the user to have Access Administrative Services permissions.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the snapshot. May be an incomplete prefix, as long as it is unambiguous.

Triggers computation of the comparison between two snapshots and returns the ID of the new comparison. One of the snapshots is stored locally, the other fetched from a remote instance.
The API requires the user to have Access Administrative Services permissions.

parameters.description: The ID of the local snapshot.

remote-access-token.description: Access token for the remote instance.

remote-snapshot-id.description: The ID of the remote snapshot. May be empty or an incomplete prefix, as long as exactly one remote snapshot is uniquely identified.

remote-user.description: User name for the remote instance. May be empty, in this case locally logged in user is assumed.

get.description:
Retrieves metadata for an ABAP file stored in Teamscale, previously retrieved from some SAP System
The API requires the user to have View Project permissions on the project.

parameters.name: project
parameters.description: The project ID.

parameters.name: filePath
parameters.description: Path to the file in Teamscale.

parameters.name: t
parameters.description: This parameter can be used to pass a timestamp giving the time (in milliseconds since 1970) for which the data should be provided. This can optionally be prefixed by the name of the branch, followed by a colon.

schema.description: This parameter can be used to pass a timestamp giving the time (in milliseconds since 1970) for which the data should be provided or "HEAD" to refer to the most recent commit. This can optionally be prefixed by the name of the branch, followed by a colon (by default, we use the default branch). You can append a "p1" to resolve to the immediate parent of the most recent commit at the given branch/timestamp. p2 would resolve to the parent of the immediate parent and so on. In merge commits, the parent resolution considers only the first parent. You can append "e" to include the potential external report uploads of the commit. A parameter value that uses all features would be "master:1601637680000p1e"

parameters.description: Uniform path to retrieve files for

parameters.name: t
parameters.description: This parameter can be used to pass a timestamp giving the time (in milliseconds since 1970) for which the data should be provided. This can optionally be prefixed by the name of the branch, followed by a colon.

schema.description: This parameter can be used to pass a timestamp giving the time (in milliseconds since 1970) for which the data should be provided or "HEAD" to refer to the most recent commit. This can optionally be prefixed by the name of the branch, followed by a colon (by default, we use the default branch). You can append a "p1" to resolve to the immediate parent of the most recent commit at the given branch/timestamp. p2 would resolve to the parent of the immediate parent and so on. In merge commits, the parent resolution considers only the first parent. You can append "e" to include the potential external report uploads of the commit. A parameter value that uses all features would be "master:1601637680000p1e"

You can append a "p1" to resolve to the immediate parent of the most recent commit at the given branch/timestamp. p2 would resolve to the parent of the immediate parent and so on. In merge commits, the parent resolution considers only the first parent. You can append "e" to include the potential external report uploads of the commit. A parameter value that uses all features would be "master:1601637680000p1e"

schema.description: This parameter can be used to pass a timestamp giving the time (in milliseconds since 1970) for which the data should be provided or "HEAD" to refer to the most recent commit. In merge commits, the parent resolution considers only the first parent. You can append "e" to include the potential external report uploads of the commit. A parameter value that uses all features would be "master:1601637680000p1e"

get.description:
Stores a clang-tidy execution setup for the given uniform path in the given directory.
The API requires the user to have Access Administrative Services permissions.

parameters.name: project
parameters.description: The project ID.

parameters.name: targetDir
parameters.description: The directory in which the service will store the execution environment. All current files in the directory will be deleted. The service requires admin permission since this can be abused.

parameters.name: t
parameters.description: This parameter can be used to pass a timestamp giving the time (in milliseconds since 1970) for which the data should be provided. This can optionally be prefixed by the name of the branch, followed by a colon.

schema.description: This parameter can be used to pass a timestamp giving the time (in milliseconds since 1970) for which the data should be provided or "HEAD" to refer to the most recent commit. This can optionally be prefixed by the name of the branch, followed by a colon (by default, we use the default branch). You can append a "p1" to resolve to the immediate parent of the most recent commit at the given branch/timestamp. p2 would resolve to the parent of the immediate parent and so on. In merge commits, the parent resolution considers only the first parent. You can append "e" to include the potential external report uploads of the commit. A parameter value that uses all features would be "master:1601637680000p1e"

parameters.name: issue-id
parameters.description: If this parameter is given and is a valid issue ID, information about all methods changed in the context of this issue ID will be returned. In this case, the baseline parameter is ignored (the baseline is determined automatically for the issue instead). If the special value "###no-connector###|[no-issue]" is given, instead all methods that were changed in commits without an issue link between the baseline and end date are returned.

parameters.name: merge-request-mode
parameters.description: If this is true, we will compute TGA information based on the methods changed in the history of commit1 but not in the history of commit2. This simulates a merge from commit1 to commit2.

parameters.name: merge-base-cache-key
parameters.description: Optional key into the cache index for merge base calculation. If this is known it can be used to speed up calculation of the merge base.

parameters.name: merge-request-identifier
parameters.description: Optional merge request identifier to retrieve the merge base info. If this is provided it can be used to directly fetch the merge base info from the merge request delta index before trying to calculate the merge base. This is especially required when creating the TgaBranchMergeRequest for an already merged  merge request.

schema.description: A merge request identifier, which consists of a connector ID and a merge request number seperated by a slash

parameters.name: auto-select-branch
parameters.description: Will auto-select the issue branch if set to true. Otherwise, if given, the branchName will be used. As fallback the default branch will be used.

parameters.name: branch-name
parameters.description: If this is given, all issues are shown on this branch. Otherwise, the branch is auto-determined for each issue separately.

parameters.name: include-child-issues
parameters.description: Will include changes introduced by child issues.

parameters.name: all-partitions
parameters.description: If this is true, all available test coverage partitions are considered.

parameters.name: partitions
parameters.description: This parameter may be given multiple times. Specifies the test coverage partitions to consider.

parameters.name: cross-annotation-project-patterns
parameters.description: This parameter may be given multiple times. Specifies further Teamscale projects from which to consider test coverage.

You can append a "p1" to resolve to the immediate parent of the most recent commit at the given branch/timestamp. p2 would resolve to the parent of the immediate parent and so on. In merge commits, the parent resolution considers only the first parent. You can append "e" to include the potential external report uploads of the commit. A parameter value that uses all features would be "master:1601637680000p1e"

A parameter value that uses all features would be "master:1601637680000p1e"

parameters.description: The project ID.

parameters.description: The partitions that should be deleted. If not set, commits are deleted from all partitions.

parameters.description: The branches where external analysis uploads should be deleted. If not set, commits are deleted from all branches.

parameters.description: The timestamp after external analysis uploads should be deleted (inclusive). If not set, the oldest entries are deleted, otherwise the entries that are between start and end are deleted.

parameters.description: The timestamp before external analysis uploads should be deleted (inclusive). If not set, the newest entries are deleted, otherwise the entries that are between start and end are deleted

schema.description: This parameter can be used to pass a timestamp giving the time (in milliseconds since 1970) for which the data should be provided or "HEAD" to refer to the most recent commit. This can optionally be prefixed by the name of the branch, followed by a colon (by default, we use the default branch). You can append a "p1" to resolve to the immediate parent of the most recent commit at the given branch/timestamp. p2 would resolve to the parent of the immediate parent and so on. In merge commits, the parent resolution considers only the first parent. You can append "e" to include the potential external report uploads of the commit. A parameter value that uses all features would be "master:1601637680000p1e"

parameters.name: revision
parameters.description: This parameter allows to pass a revision instead of a timestamp.

parameters.name: repository
parameters.description: When using the revision parameter, this parameter allows to pass a repository ID (see connectors in project) which is used to identify the correct commit in situations where the same revision exists in multiple repositories.

parameters.name: message
parameters.description: A message that describes the external analysis, similar to a commit message.

parameters.name: partition
parameters.description: The name of the logical partition to store the results into. All existing data in this partition will be invalidated. A partition typically corresponds to one analysis run, i.e. if there are two independent builds/runs, they must use different partitions.The partition parameter is only required with an auto-create session.

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: The format of the uploaded report.

parameters.name: path-prefix
parameters.description: The path prefix for the uploaded test artifacts. For coverage reports the covered paths are only matched against those files that have the specified prefix within the Teamscale project. This can be used if the same package structures and classes appear in multiple subfolders of a project. For test execution reports the prefix is prepended to the test execution names, which allows to make otherwise ambiguous test paths unique.

report.description:
The external analysis report(s) to upload to Teamscale. The reports are expected to be UTF-8 encoded, other encodings need to be specified.

Note: Files that are *not* encoded in UTF-8, or without a BOM, and are not specified as such, cannot be read reliably, which might lead to parsing errors. For more information on uploading external analysis reports, see https://docs.teamscale.com/howto/uploading-external-data.

get.summary: Get all external analysis upload commits for the specified timeframe and partitions
get.description:
Returns status infos for all external analysis upload commits in the given partitions which occurred in the given timeframe. The relevant commits are collected by going back from the given end commit through all of its predecessors until the given baseline timestamp is reached.
The API requires the user to have View Project permissions on the project.

parameters.name: project
parameters.description: The project ID.

parameters.name: baseline
parameters.description: Baseline commit. We only use this for the timestamp. Only upload commits after this timestamp will be considered.

schema.description: This parameter can be used to pass a timestamp giving the time (in milliseconds since 1970) for which the data should be provided or "HEAD" to refer to the most recent commit. This can optionally be prefixed by the name of the branch, followed by a colon (by default, we use the default branch). You can append a "p1" to resolve to the immediate parent of the most recent commit at the given branch/timestamp. p2 would resolve to the parent of the immediate parent and so on. In merge commits, the parent resolution considers only the first parent. You can append "e" to include the potential external report uploads of the commit. A parameter value that uses all features would be "master:1601637680000p1e"

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: End commit. Only upload commits which are reachable via predecessors of this commit will be considered

schema.description: This parameter can be used to pass a timestamp giving the time (in milliseconds since 1970) for which the data should be provided or "HEAD" to refer to the most recent commit. This can optionally be prefixed by the name of the branch, followed by a colon (by default, we use the default branch). You can append a "p1" to resolve to the immediate parent of the most recent commit at the given branch/timestamp. p2 would resolve to the parent of the immediate parent and so on. In merge commits, the parent resolution considers only the first parent. You can append "e" to include the potential external report uploads of the commit. A parameter value that uses all features would be "master:1601637680000p1e"

parameters.name: partitions
parameters.description: This parameter may be given multiple times. Specifies the test coverage partitions to consider.

parameters.name: all-partitions
parameters.description: If this is true, all available test coverage partitions are considered.

The API requires the user to have View Project permissions on the project.

schema.description: The project ID.

get.description:
Returns architecture upload info for the specified commit.
The API requires the user to have View Project permissions on the project.

parameters.name: project
parameters.description: The project ID.

parameters.name: commit
parameters.description: This parameter can be used to pass a timestamp giving the time (in milliseconds since 1970) for which the data should be provided. This can optionally be prefixed by the name of the branch, followed by a colon.

schema.description: This parameter can be used to pass a timestamp giving the time (in milliseconds since 1970) for which the data should be provided or "HEAD" to refer to the most recent commit. This can optionally be prefixed by the name of the branch, followed by a colon (by default, we use the default branch). You can append a "p1" to resolve to the immediate parent of the most recent commit at the given branch/timestamp. p2 would resolve to the parent of the immediate parent and so on. In merge commits, the parent resolution considers only the first parent. You can append "e" to include the potential external report uploads of the commit. A parameter value that uses all features would be "master:1601637680000p1e"

204.description: The commit does not exist.

401.description: No authentication header was provided or the credentials were invalid.

403.description: The user is authenticated, but does not have the necessary permissions to access the endpoint.

get.description:
Provides the findings churn (i.e. the list of added and deleted findings) for a given commit.
This service is public API since Teamscale 5.6.4.
The API requires the user to have View Project permissions on the project.

parameters.name: project
parameters.description: The project ID.

parameters.name: t
parameters.description: This parameter can be used to pass a timestamp giving the time (in milliseconds since 1970) for which the data should be provided. This can optionally be prefixed by the name of the branch, followed by a colon.

schema.description: This parameter can be used to pass a timestamp giving the time (in milliseconds since 1970) for which the data should be provided or "HEAD" to refer to the most recent commit. This can optionally be prefixed by the name of the branch, followed by a colon (by default, we use the default branch). You can append a "p1" to resolve to the immediate parent of the most recent commit at the given branch/timestamp. p2 would resolve to the parent of the immediate parent and so on. In merge commits, the parent resolution considers only the first parent. You can append "e" to include the potential external report uploads of the commit. A parameter value that uses all features would be "master:1601637680000p1e"

parameters.name: max
parameters.description: Maximum number of findings to return per category. If set to 0 all entries will be omitted and only relevant metadata is returned. If set to a negative value, all relevant entries are returned.

get.description:
Returns all findings for the given project.
This service is public API since Teamscale 5.6.
The API requires the user to have View Project permissions on the project.

parameters.name: project
parameters.description: The project ID.

parameters.name: regex
parameters.description: The case-insensitive regex filter. Filters findings by this regex. Considered Fields are Message and Location. Matches will be included in the result.

schema.description: A Java regex pattern

parameters.name: guideline
parameters.description: The guideline filter. Filters findings showing only findings which are relevant for this guideline.

parameters.name: guideline-rules
parameters.description: The guideline filter. Filters findings showing only findings which are relevant for this guideline.

parameters.name: exclude-regex
parameters.description: Whether regex excludes or includes findings.

parameters.name: added-to-task
parameters.description: The added to task filter. Filter findings that already have a task assigned.

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description: The finding category, group, and type filters. Every string must be either a single category, a combination category/group, or a type ID. If a category or group is given, all matching findings will be filtered out and not included in the result.

parameters.name: invert
parameters.description: Whether to invert the category, group, type filters, i.e. including the elements given in the filters instead of excluding them.

parameters.name: assessment-filters
parameters.description: The assessment filter. All mentioned assessment colors will be filtered out and not included in the result.

parameters.name: blacklisted
parameters.description: Allows to filter findings that have been tolerated and/or marked as false positives.

parameters.name: pending-exclusions
parameters.description: Further specifies whether pending finding exclusions should be included/excluded

parameters.name: included-paths
parameters.description: If this parameter is given, only findings whose uniform path matches one of the given Ant patterns are returned.

parameters.name: excluded-paths
parameters.description: If this parameter is given, only findings whose uniform path does not match one of the given Ant patterns are returned.

parameters.name: only-spec-item-findings
parameters.description: If this is true, only spec item findings are to be shown.

parameters.name: t
parameters.description: This parameter can be used to pass a timestamp giving the time (in milliseconds since 1970) for which the data should be provided. This can optionally be prefixed by the name of the branch, followed by a colon.

schema.description: This parameter can be used to pass a timestamp giving the time (in milliseconds since 1970) for which the data should be provided or "HEAD" to refer to the most recent commit. This can optionally be prefixed by the name of the branch, followed by a colon (by default, we use the default branch). You can append a "p1" to resolve to the immediate parent of the most recent commit at the given branch/timestamp. p2 would resolve to the parent of the immediate parent and so on. In merge commits, the parent resolution considers only the first parent. You can append "e" to include the potential external report uploads of the commit. A parameter value that uses all features would be "master:1601637680000p1e"

parameters.name: spec-item-query
parameters.description: If this parameter is given, only findings for spec items matching the query are returned.

parameters.name: baseline
parameters.description: The baseline name or baseline timestamp, with regards to which the findings shall be filtered.

schema.description: A baseline given either as a timestamp in milliseconds since 1970 or as the name of a baseline

parameters.name: qualified-name
parameters.description: If this parameter is given, general findings and such covering the specified qualified location are returned.

parameters.name: include-changed-code-findings
parameters.description: If this is true, findings in changed code as included as well. Only used if a baseline is provided as well.

parameters.name: only-changed-code-findings
parameters.description: If this is true, only findings in changed code are to be shown. Only used if a baseline is provided as well.

parameters.name: blacklistRationale
parameters.description: A pattern to be matched against the rationale for which the findings was tolerated or marked as false positive.

schema.description: A Java regex pattern

parameters.name: custom-issue-query
parameters.description: A custom issue query to be executed. If provided, only findings of files which were changed in at least one of the resulting issues are returned.

parameters.name: stored-issue-query
parameters.description: The name of a stored issue query to be executed. If provided, only findings of files which were changed in at least one of the resulting issues are returned.

parameters.name: sort-by
parameters.description: The finding property by which the result is sorted. If none is given the result is not sorted. One of group, uniformpath, message, or a finding property, or random

parameters.name: sort-order
parameters.description: The sort order

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: If this parameter is given, the findings returned will start from this index (0 based), i.e. the first start findings in the list (for current sorting) will be skipped.

parameters.name: max
parameters.description: Limits the number of findings returned. If also the parameter 'all' is used, the limit is ignored. Providing no limit will use the default limit of 300.

parameters.name: all
parameters.description: If this is true, the finding list is not truncated to 300 elements.

400.description: One of the filter options is invalid.

401.description: No authentication header was provided or the credentials were invalid.

403.description: The user is authenticated, but does not have the necessary permissions to access the endpoint.

get.description:
Returns findings for an ABAP source object, function group, or project. The element is expected in the form OBJTYPE/OBJNAME, e.g. 'PROG/ZTEST' or 'FUGR/!ABC!MY_FUNCTION_GROUP'.
This service is public API since Teamscale 5.6.
The API requires the user to have View Project permissions on the project.

parameters.name: project
parameters.description: The project ID.

parameters.name: objectName
parameters.description:
Object name in Teamscale-specific encoding:
Replace '!' with '#excl#', '/' with '!', '\' with '#rsol#',  ':' with '#colon#',
'<' with '#lt#', '>' with '#gt#', '|' with '#verbar#', '?' with '#quest#',
'*' with '#ast#', '"' with '#quot#', and ` ` with '#space#'. For example,"!CQSE!CL_ABAP_UTIL".

post.description:
This service allows to schedule a job that performs a cleanup of the findings identification index, removing all references to findings that do not exist (anymore). 
The API requires the user to have Access Administrative Services permissions.

parameters.name: project
parameters.description: The project ID.

parameters.name: dry-run
parameters.description: If this is provided, only a dry run is performed. In this case, only statistics are collected, without deleting any actual data.

get.description:
This is a debugging endpoint that runs a couple of consistency checks on the findings and tracked findings of a life instance and reports the results in textual form. Calling this service can potentially be very slow on large instances.
The API requires the user to have Access Administrative Services permissions.

parameters.name: project
parameters.description: The project ID.

parameters.name: full-history
parameters.description: If this is set to true, not only the head revision, but all revisions will be checked (very slow!).

get.description:
Returns all flagged findings for a given project.
The API requires the user to have View Project permissions on the project.

parameters.name: project
parameters.description: The project ID.

parameters.name: merge-base-cache-key
parameters.description: Key of a precomputed (using the "merge-requests/parent-info" endpoint) merge-base info. Should be provided in case the finding delta is requested for a (possible) merge request.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Commit denoting the start of the flagged finding interval, or the source commit in case of a (possible) merge. Must be present when "merge-base-cache-key" is provided.

schema.description: This parameter can be used to pass a timestamp giving the time (in milliseconds since 1970) for which the data should be provided or "HEAD" to refer to the most recent commit. This can optionally be prefixed by the name of the branch, followed by a colon (by default, we use the default branch). You can append a "p1" to resolve to the immediate parent of the most recent commit at the given branch/timestamp. p2 would resolve to the parent of the immediate parent and so on. In merge commits, the parent resolution considers only the first parent. You can append "e" to include the potential external report uploads of the commit. A parameter value that uses all features would be "master:1601637680000p1e"

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: Commit denoting the end of the flagged finding interval, or the target commit in case of a (possible) merge.

get.description:
Gets a list of all findings. Findings can be filtered by path prefix. For recursive queries, these are all findings found in the sub-tree. Filter parameters allow to only retrieve findings in a specific category and/or group. There is an upper limit for the number of returned findings.
This service is public API since Teamscale 5.6.
The API requires the user to have View Project permissions on the project.

parameters.name: project
parameters.description: The project ID.

parameters.name: uniform-path
parameters.description: The uniform path for which findings should be retrieved. This can be either a concrete file or a container.

parameters.name: case-insensitive-path
parameters.description: If this is true, the uniform path will be treated case-insensitive. This may be used if the file casing may be different to the one on the Teamscale server.

parameters.name: regex
parameters.description: The case-insensitive regex filter. Filters findings by this regex. Considered Fields are Message and Location. Matches will be included in the result.

schema.description: A Java regex pattern

parameters.name: guideline
parameters.description: The guideline filter. Filters findings showing only findings which are relevant for this guideline.

parameters.name: guideline-rules
parameters.description: The guideline filter. Filters findings showing only findings which are relevant for this guideline.

parameters.name: exclude-regex
parameters.description: Whether regex excludes or includes findings.

parameters.name: added-to-task
parameters.description: The added to task filter. Filter findings that already have a task assigned.

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description: The finding category, group, and type filters. Every string must be either a single category, a combination category/group, or a type ID. If a category or group is given, all matching findings will be filtered out and not included in the result.

parameters.name: invert
parameters.description: Whether to invert the category, group, type filters, i.e. including the elements given in the filters instead of excluding them.

parameters.name: assessment-filters
parameters.description: The assessment filter. All mentioned assessment colors will be filtered out and not included in the result.

parameters.name: blacklisted
parameters.description: Allows to filter findings that have been tolerated and/or marked as false positives.

parameters.name: pending-exclusions
parameters.description: Further specifies whether pending finding exclusions should be included/excluded

parameters.name: included-paths
parameters.description: If this parameter is given, only findings whose uniform path matches one of the given Ant patterns are returned.

parameters.name: excluded-paths
parameters.description: If this parameter is given, only findings whose uniform path does not match one of the given Ant patterns are returned.

parameters.name: only-spec-item-findings
parameters.description: If this is true, only spec item findings are to be shown.

parameters.name: t
parameters.description: This parameter can be used to pass a timestamp giving the time (in milliseconds since 1970) for which the data should be provided. This can optionally be prefixed by the name of the branch, followed by a colon.

schema.description: This parameter can be used to pass a timestamp giving the time (in milliseconds since 1970) for which the data should be provided or "HEAD" to refer to the most recent commit. This can optionally be prefixed by the name of the branch, followed by a colon (by default, we use the default branch). You can append a "p1" to resolve to the immediate parent of the most recent commit at the given branch/timestamp. p2 would resolve to the parent of the immediate parent and so on. In merge commits, the parent resolution considers only the first parent. You can append "e" to include the potential external report uploads of the commit. A parameter value that uses all features would be "master:1601637680000p1e"

parameters.name: spec-item-query
parameters.description: If this parameter is given, only findings for spec items matching the query are returned.

parameters.name: baseline
parameters.description: The baseline name or baseline timestamp, with regards to which the findings shall be filtered.

schema.description: A baseline given either as a timestamp in milliseconds since 1970 or as the name of a baseline

parameters.name: qualified-name
parameters.description: If this parameter is given, general findings and such covering the specified qualified location are returned.

parameters.name: include-changed-code-findings
parameters.description: If this is true, findings in changed code as included as well. Only used if a baseline is provided as well.

parameters.name: only-changed-code-findings
parameters.description: If this is true, only findings in changed code are to be shown. Only used if a baseline is provided as well.

parameters.name: blacklistRationale
parameters.description: A pattern to be matched against the rationale for which the findings was tolerated or marked as false positive.

schema.description: A Java regex pattern

parameters.name: custom-issue-query
parameters.description: A custom issue query to be executed. If provided, only findings of files which were changed in at least one of the resulting issues are returned.

parameters.name: stored-issue-query
parameters.description: The name of a stored issue query to be executed. If provided, only findings of files which were changed in at least one of the resulting issues are returned.

parameters.name: sort-by
parameters.description: The finding property by which the result is sorted. If none is given the result is not sorted. One of group, uniformpath, message, or a finding property, or random

parameters.name: sort-order
parameters.description: The sort order

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: If this parameter is given, the findings returned will start from this index (0 based), i.e. the first start findings in the list (for current sorting) will be skipped.

parameters.name: max
parameters.description: Limits the number of findings returned. If also the parameter 'all' is used, the limit is ignored. Providing no limit will use the default limit of 300.

parameters.name: all
parameters.description: If this is true, the finding list is not truncated to 300 elements.

400.description: One of the filter options is invalid.

401.description: No authentication header was provided or the credentials were invalid.

403.description: The user is authenticated, but does not have the necessary permissions to access the endpoint.

This service is public API since Teamscale 6.0.
The API requires the user to have View Project permissions on the project.

parameters.name: project
parameters.description: The project ID.

parameters.name: t
parameters.description: This parameter can be used to pass a timestamp giving the time (in milliseconds since 1970) for which the data should be provided. This can optionally be prefixed by the name of the branch, followed by a colon.

schema.description: This parameter can be used to pass a timestamp giving the time (in milliseconds since 1970) for which the data should be provided or "HEAD" to refer to the most recent commit. This can optionally be prefixed by the name of the branch, followed by a colon (by default, we use the default branch). You can append a "p1" to resolve to the immediate parent of the most recent commit at the given branch/timestamp. p2 would resolve to the parent of the immediate parent and so on. In merge commits, the parent resolution considers only the first parent. You can append "e" to include the potential external report uploads of the commit. A parameter value that uses all features would be "master:1601637680000p1e"

parameters.name: only-spec-item-findings
parameters.description: If this is true, only spec item findings are to be shown.

400.description: One of the filter options is invalid.

401.description: No authentication header was provided or the credentials were invalid.

403.description: The user is authenticated, but does not have the necessary permissions to access the endpoint.

get.description:
Gets next and prev finding respecting filter and sort options. 
The API requires the user to have View Project permissions on the project.

parameters.name: project
parameters.description: The project ID.

parameters.name: uniform-path
parameters.description: The uniform path for which findings should be retrieved. This can be either a concrete file or a container.

parameters.name: case-insensitive-path
parameters.description: If this is true, the uniform path will be treated case-insensitive. This may be used if the file casing may be different to the one on the Teamscale server.

parameters.name: regex
parameters.description: The case-insensitive regex filter. Filters findings by this regex. Considered Fields are Message and Location. Matches will be included in the result.

schema.description: A Java regex pattern

parameters.name: guideline
parameters.description: The guideline filter. Filters findings showing only findings which are relevant for this guideline.

parameters.name: guideline-rules
parameters.description: The guideline filter. Filters findings showing only findings which are relevant for this guideline.

parameters.name: exclude-regex
parameters.description: Whether regex excludes or includes findings.

parameters.name: added-to-task
parameters.description: The added to task filter. Filter findings that already have a task assigned.

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description: The finding category, group, and type filters. Every string must be either a single category, a combination category/group, or a type ID. If a category or group is given, all matching findings will be filtered out and not included in the result.

parameters.name: invert
parameters.description: Whether to invert the category, group, type filters, i.e. including the elements given in the filters instead of excluding them.

parameters.name: assessment-filters
parameters.description: The assessment filter. All mentioned assessment colors will be filtered out and not included in the result.

parameters.name: blacklisted
parameters.description: Allows to filter findings that have been tolerated and/or marked as false positives.

parameters.name: pending-exclusions
parameters.description: Further specifies whether pending finding exclusions should be included/excluded

parameters.name: included-paths
parameters.description: If this parameter is given, only findings whose uniform path matches one of the given Ant patterns are returned.

parameters.name: excluded-paths
parameters.description: If this parameter is given, only findings whose uniform path does not match one of the given Ant patterns are returned.

parameters.name: only-spec-item-findings
parameters.description: If this is true, only spec item findings are to be shown.

parameters.name: t
parameters.description: This parameter can be used to pass a timestamp giving the time (in milliseconds since 1970) for which the data should be provided. This can optionally be prefixed by the name of the branch, followed by a colon.

schema.description: This parameter can be used to pass a timestamp giving the time (in milliseconds since 1970) for which the data should be provided or "HEAD" to refer to the most recent commit. This can optionally be prefixed by the name of the branch, followed by a colon (by default, we use the default branch). You can append a "p1" to resolve to the immediate parent of the most recent commit at the given branch/timestamp. p2 would resolve to the parent of the immediate parent and so on. In merge commits, the parent resolution considers only the first parent. You can append "e" to include the potential external report uploads of the commit. A parameter value that uses all features would be "master:1601637680000p1e"

parameters.name: spec-item-query
parameters.description: If this parameter is given, only findings for spec items matching the query are returned.

parameters.name: baseline
parameters.description: The baseline name or baseline timestamp, with regards to which the findings shall be filtered.

schema.description: A baseline given either as a timestamp in milliseconds since 1970 or as the name of a baseline

parameters.name: qualified-name
parameters.description: If this parameter is given, general findings and such covering the specified qualified location are returned.

parameters.name: include-changed-code-findings
parameters.description: If this is true, findings in changed code as included as well. Only used if a baseline is provided as well.

parameters.name: only-changed-code-findings
parameters.description: If this is true, only findings in changed code are to be shown. Only used if a baseline is provided as well.

parameters.name: blacklistRationale
parameters.description: A pattern to be matched against the rationale for which the findings was tolerated or marked as false positive.

schema.description: A Java regex pattern

parameters.name: custom-issue-query
parameters.description: A custom issue query to be executed. If provided, only findings of files which were changed in at least one of the resulting issues are returned.

parameters.name: stored-issue-query
parameters.description: The name of a stored issue query to be executed. If provided, only findings of files which were changed in at least one of the resulting issues are returned.

parameters.name: sort-by
parameters.description: The finding property by which the result is sorted. If none is given the result is not sorted. One of group, uniformpath, message, or a finding property, or random

parameters.name: sort-order
parameters.description: The sort order

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: If this parameter is given, the findings returned will start from this index (0 based), i.e. the first start findings in the list (for current sorting) will be skipped.

parameters.name: max
parameters.description: Limits the number of findings returned. If also the parameter 'all' is used, the limit is ignored. Providing no limit will use the default limit of 300.

parameters.name: all
parameters.description: If this is true, the finding list is not truncated to 300 elements.

400.description: One of the filter options is invalid.

401.description: No authentication header was provided or the credentials were invalid.

403.description: The user is authenticated, but does not have the necessary permissions to access the endpoint.

The API requires the user to have Access Administrative Services permissions.

post.description:
Triggers a complete debug dump of all labeling-related information during the next regularly scheduled commit tree expansion. This uses the infrastructure of the git debug dumper, which requires some environment variables to be set. Minimally, com.teamscale.debug-dump.enabled=true and com.teamscale.debug-dump.path=/some/path need to be set. Optionally, use com.teamscale.debug-dump.projects=(project1|project2) to restrict this further.
The API requires the user to have Access Administrative Services permissions.

parameters.name: project
parameters.description: The project ID.

parameters.name: repositoryId
parameters.description: The ID of the repository

400.description: The given repository ID is not valid.

401.description: No authentication header was provided or the credentials were invalid.

403.description: The user is authenticated, but does not have the necessary permissions to access the endpoint.

501.description: Debug dump requested, but debug dumper not enabled in system properties

post.description:
Cancels all check runs for the given Git refs which were posted by Teamscale and are still in progress. This does *not* cancel any ongoing pull request analyses but only concludes the preliminary check run.
The API requires the user to have Access Administrative Services permissions.

parameters.name: project
parameters.description: The project ID.

parameters.name: repository
parameters.description: The repository name consisting of the owner and the repository separated by a slash.

parameters.name: refs
parameters.description: The Git refs whose check runs should be cancelled. Can be a commit SHA, branch name (heads/\<branch name\>) or tag (tags/\<tag name\>).

post.summary: Cancels all in-progress check runs for a project
post.description:
Cancels all check runs for a project which were posted by Teamscale and are still in progress. This does *not* cancel any ongoing pull request analyses but only concludes the preliminary check run. Teamscale will open new check runs if required.
The API requires the user to have Access Administrative Services permissions.

get.description:
Returns an ordered list of test clusters that are impacted by the given changes. The returned flat list of tests is a subset of all tests known to Teamscale. When the tests themselves have changed, new tests were added or tests have been deleted, this service cannot take that into account. Please use the PUT endpoint instead to provide a list of available tests. In particular, this endpoint may return tests that no longer exist. Callers must handle this case.
This service is public API since Teamscale 9.4.
The API requires the user to have Retrieve Test Suggestions permissions on the project.

parameters.name: project
parameters.description: The project ID.

parameters.name: partitions
parameters.description: This parameter may be given multiple times. Specifies the test coverage partitions to consider.

parameters.name: all-partitions
parameters.description: If this is true, all available test coverage partitions are considered.

parameters.name: include-non-impacted
parameters.description: Append and prioritize non-impacted tests after impacted tests.

parameters.name: include-failed-and-skipped
parameters.description: Append and prioritize previously failed and skipped tests before impacted tests.

parameters.name: include-added-tests
parameters.description: Append and prioritize tests for which there is no coverage yet.

parameters.name: baseline
parameters.description: The baseline commit (exclusive). This parameter can be used to pass a timestamp giving the time (in milliseconds since 1970) for which the data should be provided or "HEAD" to refer to the most recent commit. This can optionally be prefixed by the name of the branch, followed by a colon (by default, we use the default branch). You can append a "p1" to resolve to the immediate parent of the most recent commit at the given branch/timestamp. p2 would resolve to the parent of the immediate parent and so on. In merge commits, the parent resolution considers only the first parent. You can append "e" to include the potential external report uploads of the commit. A parameter value that uses all features would be "master:1601637680000p1e"

You can append a "p1" to resolve to the immediate parent of the most recent commit at the given branch/timestamp. p2 would resolve to the parent of the immediate parent and so on. In merge commits, the parent resolution considers only the first parent. You can append "e" to include the potential external report uploads of the commit. parameters.name: baseline-revision

parameters.name: end-revision
parameters.description: The end revision (inclusive). If both revision and commit are given, the revision will be preferred.

parameters.name: repository
parameters.description: When using the revision parameter, this parameter allows to pass a repository name which is used to identify the correct commit in situations where the same revision exists in multiple repositories.

parameters.name: prioritization-strategy
parameters.description: The name of the test prioritization strategy

parameters.name: ensure-processed
parameters.description: If set to true the request will fail with '412 PRECONDITION FAILED' if the exact given end commit has not been processed yet and therefore data may not be up-to-date.

parameters.name: max-exec-time
parameters.description: The upper bound for the predicted test execution duration in milliseconds that the resulting test list should exhibit. All impacted tests that do not fit into this budget will be cut off.

parameters.name: covering-path
parameters.description: Consider coverage within this given uniform path only.

412.description: The given commit has not been processed yet.

put.description:
Returns an ordered list of test clusters that are impacted by the given changes. The returned list of test clusters is a subset of the tests given in the request body. Clusters of selected and prioritized tests are formed based on the clustering information given in the request body. The tests from the request body are furthermore used to determine if any tests have changed or new tests were added. These are always included in the returned list of impacted tests.
This service is public API since Teamscale 9.4.
The API requires the user to have Retrieve Test Suggestions permissions on the project.

parameters.description: The issue query

parameters.name: auto-select-branch
parameters.description: Will auto-select the issue branch if set to true. Otherwise, if given, the branchName will be used. As fallback the default branch will be used.

parameters.name: branch-name
parameters.description: If this is given, all issues are shown on this branch. Otherwise, the branch is auto-determined for each issue separately.

parameters.name: include-child-issues
parameters.description: Will include changes introduced by child issues.

parameters.name: all-partitions
parameters.description: If this is true, all available test coverage partitions are considered.

parameters.name: partitions
parameters.description: This parameter may be given multiple times. Specifies the test coverage partitions to consider.

parameters.name: cross-annotation-project-patterns
parameters.description: This parameter may be given multiple times. Specifies further Teamscale projects from which to consider test coverage.

parameters.name: execution-only
parameters.description: If this parameter is given, the service disregards code changes and only assesses execution/coverage of methods. The result will not contain information about test gaps, but only about which methods were executed.

parameters.name: churn
parameters.description: Whether we want to view only the churn. This means coverage is ignored for the assessment and methods are only classified as unchanged, added or modified.

post.description:
This service allows force pushing of the latest information available for issues into Jira. If an 'issue-id' is provided, then the specified issue will be updated. Otherwise, all issues in the project will be updated.This should only be used for debugging.
The API requires the user to have Access Administrative Services permissions.

parameters.name: project
parameters.description: The project ID.

parameters.name: issue-id
parameters.description: The format is the connector ID of the originating issue tracker/requirements management tool (as specified in the connector settings in the project configuration), followed by the separator '|', followed by the ID of the issue, as it is provided by the external issue tracker/requirements management tool. For example, an issue with the ID "TS-123" is imported using an issue tracker connector with the connector ID "issues1". The expected ID is then "issues1|TS-123". Leave empty to update all issues in the project.

The format is the connector ID of the originating issue tracker/requirements management tool (as specified in the connector settings in the project configuration), followed by the separator '|', followed by the ID of the issue, as it is provided by the external issue tracker/requirements management tool. get.description:
Builds an issue treemap for the given parameters. If the color metric is set to -1, it calculates how many issues reference the given nodes, while any other valid color metric value calculates the corresponding metric for the nodes.
The API requires the user to have View Project permissions on the project.

parameters.name: project
parameters.description: The project ID.

parameters.name: t
parameters.description: This parameter can be used to pass a timestamp giving the time (in milliseconds since 1970) for which the data should be provided. This can optionally be prefixed by the name of the branch, followed by a colon.

schema.description: This parameter can be used to pass a timestamp giving the time (in milliseconds since 1970) for which the data should be provided or "HEAD" to refer to the most recent commit. This can optionally be prefixed by the name of the branch, followed by a colon (by default, we use the default branch). You can append a "p1" to resolve to the immediate parent of the most recent commit at the given branch/timestamp. p2 would resolve to the parent of the immediate parent and so on. In merge commits, the parent resolution considers only the first parent. You can append "e" to include the potential external report uploads of the commit. A parameter value that uses all features would be "master:1601637680000p1e"

parameters.name: uniform-path
parameters.description: Uniform path to build an issue treemap for

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Issue query to build a treemap for

parameters.name: exclude-non-referenced
parameters.description: Exclude files that are not referenced by issues

parameters.name: additional-branches
parameters.description: Regular expression describing additional branches for which issue references should be loaded.

schema.description: A Java regex pattern

parameters.name: summary-type
parameters.description: Specifies how the statistics information displayed in the subtitle should be calculated.

parameters.name: violation-threshold
parameters.description: Specifies a threshold for numeric metric summaries. The percent of files/area that violate the given threshold will be displayed in the summary.

parameters.name: area-metric
parameters.description: The index for the metric used to determine the area of the displayed nodes.

parameters.name: color-metric
parameters.description: The index for the metric used to determine the color of the displayed nodes.

parameters.name: included-files-regexes
parameters.description: Regular expressions the file paths have to match to be displayed.

parameters.name: excluded-files-regexes
parameters.description: Regular expressions the file paths may not match to be displayed.

parameters.name: partition
parameters.description: The partitions to consider for metric retrieval

parameters.name: all-partitions
parameters.description: Whether all partitions should be included

parameters.name: color-blind-mode
parameters.description: Whether to enable the color-blind mode for the Treemap

get.description:
Determines an aggregated finding churn across all commits of the issue.
This service is public API since Teamscale 5.9.
The API requires the user to have View Project permissions on the project.

parameters.name: project
parameters.description: The project ID.

parameters.name: issueId
parameters.description: ID of the issue to determine the finding churn for. The format is the connector ID of the originating issue tracker/requirements management tool (as specified in the connector settings in the project configuration), followed by the separator '|', followed by the ID of the issue, as it is provided by the external issue tracker/requirements management tool. For example, an issue with the ID "TS-123" is imported using an issue tracker connector with the connector ID "issues1". The expected ID is then "issues1|TS-123". For compatibility reasons, using only the issue ID (e.g. "TS-123") is also supported but discouraged.

parameters.name: exclude-resolved-findings
parameters.description: If set, findings that have already been resolved will not be included in the result

parameters.name: exclude-flagged-findings
parameters.description: If set, findings that have been flagged as tolerated or false-positives will not be included in the result

parameters.name: max
parameters.description: Maximum number of findings to return per category. If set to 0 all entries will be omitted and only relevant metadata is returned. If set to a negative value, all relevant entries are returned.

parameters.description: The project ID.

parameters.description: ID of the issue to retrieve impacted tests for. The format is the connector ID of the originating issue tracker/requirements management tool (as specified in the connector settings in the project configuration), followed by the separator '|', followed by the ID of the issue, as it is provided by the external issue tracker/requirements management tool. For example, an issue with the ID "TS-123" is imported using an issue tracker connector with the connector ID "issues1". The expected ID is then "issues1|TS-123".

parameters.name: auto-select-branch
parameters.description: Will auto-select the issue branch if set to true. Otherwise, if given, the branchName will be used. As fallback the default branch will be used.

parameters.name: branch-name
parameters.description: If this is given, all issues are shown on this branch. Otherwise, the branch is auto-determined for each issue separately.

parameters.name: include-child-issues
parameters.description: Will include changes introduced by child issues.

parameters.name: partitions
parameters.description: This parameter may be given multiple times. Specifies the test coverage partitions to consider.

parameters.name: all-partitions
parameters.description: If this is true, all available test coverage partitions are considered.

This service is public API since Teamscale 7.6.
The API requires the user to have View Project permissions on the project.

parameters.name: project
parameters.description: The project ID.

parameters.name: issueId
parameters.description: ID of the issue to retrieve the TGA summary for. The format is the connector ID of the originating issue tracker/requirements management tool (as specified in the connector settings in the project configuration), followed by the separator '|', followed by the ID of the issue, as it is provided by the external issue tracker/requirements management tool. For example, an issue with the ID "TS-123" is imported using an issue tracker connector with the connector ID "issues1". The expected ID is then "issues1|TS-123". For compatibility reasons, using only the issue ID (e.g. "TS-123") is also supported but discouraged.

parameters.name: auto-select-branch
parameters.description: Will auto-select the issue branch if set to true. Otherwise, if given, the branchName will be used. As fallback the default branch will be used.

parameters.name: branch-name
parameters.description: If this is given, all issues are shown on this branch. Otherwise, the branch is auto-determined for each issue separately.

parameters.name: include-child-issues
parameters.description: Will include changes introduced by child issues.

parameters.name: all-partitions
parameters.description: If this is true, all available test coverage partitions are considered.

parameters.name: partitions
parameters.description: This parameter may be given multiple times. Specifies the test coverage partitions to consider.

parameters.name: cross-annotation-project-patterns
parameters.description: This parameter may be given multiple times. Specifies further Teamscale projects from which to consider test coverage.

parameters.name: execution-only
parameters.description: If this parameter is given, the service disregards code changes and only assesses execution/coverage of methods. The result will not contain information about test gaps, but only about which methods were executed.

parameters.name: churn
parameters.description: Whether we want to view only the churn. This means coverage is ignored for the assessment and methods are only classified as unchanged, added or modified.

get.description:
Returns a list of all critical event short logs.
The API requires the user to have View System Status permissions.

parameters.name: project
parameters.description: The project where the logs originate ("##maintenance##" for the maintenance project).

schema.description: The project ID.

parameters.name: minLogLevel
parameters.description: The minimum log level to retrieve log entries for. This includes all levels that are more severe than the given one.

parameters.name: maxResults
parameters.description: The maximum number of log entries to return, or 0 to return all entries.

parameters.name: startTimestamp
parameters.description: The timestamp of the earliest log entry to include. If greater than endTimestamp, sort order is inverted.

parameters.name: endTimestamp
parameters.description: The timestamp of the latest log entry to include. If smaller than endTimestamp, sort order is inverted.

parameters.name: oldestTimestamp
parameters.description: The timestamp of the oldest log entry that will be displayed. This is necessary to determine display of collapsed log entries.

parameters.name: filterRegex
parameters.description: Optional regular expression used for filtering log entries.

parameters.name: includeMatches
parameters.description: Whether to include or exclude matches of the filterRegex.

parameters.name: searchDetailedLogs
parameters.description: Whether to search detailed log messages (may be expensive)

parameters.name: collapseRepeatedEntries
parameters.description: Whether to collapse repeated entries

get.description:
Builds a treemap for matched types.
The API requires the user to have View Project permissions on the project.

parameters.name: project
parameters.description: The project ID.

parameters.name: t
parameters.description: This parameter can be used to pass a timestamp giving the time (in milliseconds since 1970) for which the data should be provided. This can optionally be prefixed by the name of the branch, followed by a colon.

schema.description: This parameter can be used to pass a timestamp giving the time (in milliseconds since 1970) for which the data should be provided or "HEAD" to refer to the most recent commit. This can optionally be prefixed by the name of the branch, followed by a colon (by default, we use the default branch). You can append a "p1" to resolve to the immediate parent of the most recent commit at the given branch/timestamp. p2 would resolve to the parent of the immediate parent and so on. In merge commits, the parent resolution considers only the first parent. You can append "e" to include the potential external report uploads of the commit. A parameter value that uses all features would be "master:1601637680000p1e"

parameters.name: uniform-path
parameters.description: Uniform path to build a treemap for

parameters.name: matchedTypes
parameters.description: Files that are matched by the component. These files will be colored in.

parameters.name: area-metric
parameters.description: The index for the metric used to determine the area of the displayed nodes.

parameters.name: color-metric
parameters.description: The index for the metric used to determine the color of the displayed nodes.

parameters.name: included-files-regexes
parameters.description: Regular expressions the file paths have to match to be displayed.

parameters.name: excluded-files-regexes
parameters.description: Regular expressions the file paths may not match to be displayed.

parameters.name: partition
parameters.description: The partitions to consider for metric retrieval

parameters.name: all-partitions
parameters.description: Whether all partitions should be included

parameters.name: color-blind-mode
parameters.description: Whether to enable the color-blind mode for the Treemap

get.description:
Creates a findings delta (newly added or deleted findings and unchanged findings in modified code) for a given proposed merge. Findings in modified code will only be reported if the modification happened since the last common commit with the target branch.
The API requires the user to have View Project permissions on the project.

parameters.name: project
parameters.description: The project ID.

parameters.name: source
parameters.description: The source commit descriptor.

schema.description: This parameter can be used to pass a timestamp giving the time (in milliseconds since 1970) for which the data should be provided or "HEAD" to refer to the most recent commit. This can optionally be prefixed by the name of the branch, followed by a colon (by default, we use the default branch). You can append a "p1" to resolve to the immediate parent of the most recent commit at the given branch/timestamp. p2 would resolve to the parent of the immediate parent and so on. In merge commits, the parent resolution considers only the first parent. You can append "e" to include the potential external report uploads of the commit. A parameter value that uses all features would be "master:1601637680000p1e"

parameters.name: target
parameters.description: The target commit descriptor.

parameters.name: merge-base-cache-key
parameters.description: Key of a precomputed merge-base info object.

parameters.name: regex
parameters.description: The case-insensitive regex filter. Filters findings by this regex. Considered Fields are Message and Location. Matches will be included in the result.

schema.description: A Java regex pattern

parameters.name: guideline
parameters.description: The guideline filter. Filters findings showing only findings which are relevant for this guideline.

parameters.name: guideline-rules
parameters.description: The guideline filter. Filters findings showing only findings which are relevant for this guideline.

parameters.name: exclude-regex
parameters.description: Whether regex excludes or includes findings.

parameters.name: added-to-task
parameters.description: The added to task filter. Filter findings that already have a task assigned.

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description: The finding category, group, and type filters. Every string must be either a single category, a combination category/group, or a type ID. If a category or group is given, all matching findings will be filtered out and not included in the result.

parameters.name: invert
parameters.description: Whether to invert the category, group, type filters, i.e. including the elements given in the filters instead of excluding them.

parameters.name: assessment-filters
parameters.description: The assessment filter. All mentioned assessment colors will be filtered out and not included in the result.

parameters.name: blacklisted
parameters.description: Allows to filter findings that have been tolerated and/or marked as false positives.

parameters.name: pending-exclusions
parameters.description: Further specifies whether pending finding exclusions should be included/excluded

parameters.name: included-paths
parameters.description: If this parameter is given, only findings whose uniform path matches one of the given Ant patterns are returned.

parameters.name: excluded-paths
parameters.description: If this parameter is given, only findings whose uniform path does not match one of the given Ant patterns are returned.

parameters.name: only-spec-item-findings
parameters.description: If this is true, only spec item findings are to be shown.

This service is public API since Teamscale 2025.6.
The API requires the user to have View Project permissions on the project.

parameters.description: The project ID.

parameters.description: Source commit descriptor. This is usually the source branch name with suffixed with ":HEAD" to refer to the the latest commit on that branch.

schema.description: This parameter can be used to pass a timestamp giving the time (in milliseconds since 1970) for which the data should be provided or "HEAD" to refer to the most recent commit. This can optionally be prefixed by the name of the branch, followed by a colon (by default, we use the default branch). A parameter value that uses all features would be "master:1601637680000p1e"

parameters.name: target

You can append a "p1" to resolve to the immediate parent of the most recent commit at the given branch/timestamp. p2 would resolve to the parent of the immediate parent and so on. post.description:
Updates the project metric schema with the entries in the analysis profile.
User must either have the permission to create analysis profiles, or the permission to view any existing analysis profile.

parameters.name: project
parameters.description: The project ID.

parameters.name: t
parameters.description: This parameter can be used to pass a timestamp giving the time (in milliseconds since 1970) for which the data should be provided. This can optionally be prefixed by the name of the branch, followed by a colon.

schema.description: This parameter can be used to pass a timestamp giving the time (in milliseconds since 1970) for which the data should be provided or "HEAD" to refer to the most recent commit. This can optionally be prefixed by the name of the branch, followed by a colon (by default, we use the default branch). You can append a "p1" to resolve to the immediate parent of the most recent commit at the given branch/timestamp. p2 would resolve to the parent of the immediate parent and so on. In merge commits, the parent resolution considers only the first parent. You can append "e" to include the potential external report uploads of the commit. A parameter value that uses all features would be "master:1601637680000p1e"

parameters.name: analysis-profile-name
parameters.description: The name of the analysis profile used to extract the enabled external metrics.

400.description: Upload was rejected, because it refers to a timestamp too far back in time.

401.description: No authentication header was provided or the credentials were invalid.

403.description: The user is authenticated, but does not have the necessary permissions to access the endpoint.

get.description:
Retrieves the metric hotspots for the provided parameters. Hotspots are determined using the aggregation information of the metric. The result is determined by ranking all elements with regards to each individual metric and computing an overall ranking by equally weighting each metric rank.
The API requires the user to have View Project permissions on the project.

parameters.name: project
parameters.description: The project ID.

parameters.name: t
parameters.description: This parameter can be used to pass a timestamp giving the time (in milliseconds since 1970) for which the data should be provided. This can optionally be prefixed by the name of the branch, followed by a colon.

schema.description: This parameter can be used to pass a timestamp giving the time (in milliseconds since 1970) for which the data should be provided or "HEAD" to refer to the most recent commit. This can optionally be prefixed by the name of the branch, followed by a colon (by default, we use the default branch). You can append a "p1" to resolve to the immediate parent of the most recent commit at the given branch/timestamp. p2 would resolve to the parent of the immediate parent and so on. In merge commits, the parent resolution considers only the first parent. You can append "e" to include the potential external report uploads of the commit. A parameter value that uses all features would be "master:1601637680000p1e"

parameters.name: uniform-path
parameters.description: Uniform path to compute metric hotspots for

parameters.name: metric-indexes
parameters.description: Metric indexes to compute metric hotspots for

parameters.name: num-results
parameters.description: Maximum result count to return

parameters.name: score-cutoff
parameters.description: The score at which to perform a cutoff (i.e., files with this score or higher will no longer be displayed)

400.description: Hotspots cannot be determined for non-numeric metrics.

401.description: No authentication header was provided or the credentials were invalid.

403.description: The user is authenticated, but does not have the necessary permissions to access the endpoint.
