get.description:
Returns a list of test clusters that have been determined to be the best tests to run in the amount of time that was specified in the request. The clusters are formed according tothe clustering-regex that can be specified in the request options.
This service is public API since Teamscale 7.8.
The API requires the user to have Retrieve Test Suggestions permissions on the project.

parameters.name: project
parameters.description: The project ID.

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: The end commit (inclusive). This parameter can be used to pass a timestamp giving the time (in milliseconds since 1970) for which the data should be provided or "HEAD" to refer to the most recent commit. This can optionally be prefixed by the name of the branch, followed by a colon (by default, we use the default branch). You can append a "p1" to resolve to the immediate parent of the most recent commit at the given branch/timestamp. p2 would resolve to the parent of the immediate parent and so on. In merge commits, the parent resolution considers only the first parent. You can append "e" to include the potential external report uploads of the commit. A parameter value that uses all features would be "master:1601637680000p1e"

schema.description: This parameter can be used to pass a timestamp giving the time (in milliseconds since 1970) for which the data should be provided or "HEAD" to refer to the most recent commit. This can optionally be prefixed by the name of the branch, followed by a colon (by default, we use the default branch). You can append a "p1" to resolve to the immediate parent of the most recent commit at the given branch/timestamp. p2 would resolve to the parent of the immediate parent and so on. In merge commits, the parent resolution considers only the first parent. You can append "e" to include the potential external report uploads of the commit. A parameter value that uses all features would be "master:1601637680000p1e"

parameters.name: partitions
parameters.description: This parameter may be given multiple times. Specifies the test coverage partitions to consider.

parameters.name: ensure-processed
parameters.description: If set to true the request will fail with '412 PRECONDITION FAILED' if the exact given end commit has not been processed yet and therefore data may not be up-to-date.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Do the minimization only for the tests specified by the query.

parameters.name: max-exec-time
parameters.description: The maximum duration of the minimized test suite in milliseconds (only relevant for test suite minimization).

parameters.name: clustering-regex
parameters.description: The regex that is used to determine the clustering of tests. The first capture group of the regex should match the subset of the test uniform path that represents the cluster ID. Tests with the same cluster ID are grouped and prioritized together. If left empty, each cluster contains exactly one test.

schema.description: A Java regex pattern

parameters.name: covering-path
parameters.description: Consider coverage within this given uniform path only.

400.description: One of the given options is invalid.

401.description: No authentication header was provided or the credentials were invalid.

403.description: The user is authenticated, but does not have the necessary permissions to access the endpoint.

412.description: The given commit has not been processed yet.

parameters.name: project
parameters.description: The project ID.

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: The end commit (inclusive). This parameter can be used to pass a timestamp giving the time (in milliseconds since 1970) for which the data should be provided or "HEAD" to refer to the most recent commit. This can optionally be prefixed by the name of the branch, followed by a colon (by default, we use the default branch). You can append a "p1" to resolve to the immediate parent of the most recent commit at the given branch/timestamp. p2 would resolve to the parent of the immediate parent and so on. In merge commits, the parent resolution considers only the first parent. You can append "e" to include the potential external report uploads of the commit. A parameter value that uses all features would be "master:1601637680000p1e"

parameters.name: max-exec-time
parameters.description: The maximum duration of the minimized test suite in milliseconds (only relevant for test suite minimization).

parameters.name: clustering-regex
parameters.description: The regex that is used to determine the clustering of tests. The first capture group of the regex should match the subset of the test uniform path that represents the cluster ID. Tests with the same cluster ID are grouped and prioritized together. If left empty, each cluster contains exactly one test.

schema.description: A Java regex pattern

parameters.name: covering-path
parameters.description: Consider coverage within this given uniform path only.

get.summary: Get risk methods along with an origin of the risk
get.description:
Returns all methods that are considered a risk and should therefore be covered with tests. These are either risks because there were changes to the methods in the given time range, or these methods were changed in context of the given issues (defined by an issue query). Along with each method, we provide the last timestamps (long values) of changes and the issue ids in context of that these changes were made.
The API requires the user to have Retrieve Test Suggestions permissions on the project.

parameters.name: only-executed-methods
parameters.description: If this parameter is given, the service disregards code changes and only assesses execution of methods.

parameters.name: uniform-path
parameters.description: Uniform path at which the request should focus on

parameters.name: test-uniform-path
parameters.description: The test uniform path for which coverage should be shown in the treemap. This is an exclusive alternative to test-query i.e. if given, test-query is ignored.

parameters.name: test-query
parameters.description: Test query that determines which coverage should be shown in the treemap. This is an exclusive alternative to test-uniform-path i.e. if test-uniform-path is given, this parameter is ignored.

parameters.name: issue-id
parameters.description: If this parameter is given and is a valid issue ID, information about all methods changed in the context of this issue ID will be returned. In this case, the baseline parameter is ignored (the baseline is determined automatically for the issue instead). If the special value "###no-connector###|[no-issue]" is given, instead all methods that were changed in commits without an issue link between the baseline and end date are returned.

parameters.name: merge-request-mode
parameters.description: If this is true, we will compute TGA information based on the methods changed in the history of commit1 but not in the history of commit2. This simulates a merge from commit1 to commit2.

parameters.name: merge-base-cache-key
parameters.description: Optional key into the cache index for merge base calculation. If this is known it can be used to speed up calculation of the merge base.

parameters.name: merge-request-identifier
parameters.description: Optional merge request identifier to retrieve the merge base info. If this is provided it can be used to directly fetch the merge base info from the merge request delta index before trying to calculate the merge base. This is especially required when creating the TgaBranchMergeRequest for an already merged  merge request.

schema.description: A merge request identifier, which consists of a connector ID and a merge request number seperated by a slash

parameters.name: execution-only
parameters.description: If this parameter is given, the service disregards code changes and only assesses execution/coverage of methods. The result will not contain information about test gaps, but only about which methods were executed.

parameters.name: churn
parameters.description: Whether we want to view only the churn. This means coverage is ignored for the assessment and methods are only classified as unchanged, added or modified.

put.description:
Uploads new change data and analyzes them on a user-specific pre-commit branch. The new content is uploaded as a map from path to new/changed content. To mark deleted files (also after a rename), put null values into the map. The service returns immediately with a list of already calculated findings and an optional pre-commit token that can be used to poll for an updated list of findings given the new file contents. The initial finding response contains already adjusted finding locations with regards to the passed content, this means no location adjustment is needed on the client side. If all file contents match the content on the server, no pre-commit polling token is returned. If pre-commit limits are exceeded, an optional error message is returned, but the current findings are returned as well.
This service is public API since Teamscale 6.7.
The API requires the user to have View Project permissions on the project.

parameters.name: project
parameters.description: The project ID.

parameters.name: branch
parameters.description: The repository branch that this precommit-request is based on (the branch that the user works on when starting this request)

requestBody.description: The paths and content of added, changed or deleted files. Files are considered deleted if null content is given.

200.description: Returns the preliminary pre-commit result. Any errors are encoded in the 'error' field of the response.

get.description:
Returns debugging information on a preprocessing operation. This uses our own C/C++ preprocessor and only works for languages using this preprocessor. The service first preprocesses all contents of a given file up to a given line (in particular #include and #define directives). Then it preprocesses a given code snippet using all defines gathered during the file preprocessing. The intermediate steps of the preprocessor during this second expansion are returned including information about included/unresolved files and defined/used macros.
The API requires the user to have View Project permissions on the project.

parameters.name: project
parameters.description: The project ID.

parameters.name: t
parameters.description: This parameter can be used to pass a timestamp giving the time (in milliseconds since 1970) for which the data should be provided. This can optionally be prefixed by the name of the branch, followed by a colon.

This can optionally be prefixed by the name of the branch, followed by a colon (by default, we use the default branch). You can append a "p1" to resolve to the immediate parent of the most recent commit at the given branch/timestamp. p2 would resolve to the parent of the immediate parent and so on. In merge commits, the parent resolution considers only the first parent. You can append "e" to include the potential external report uploads of the commit. A parameter value that uses all features would be "master:1601637680000p1e"

parameters.name: code
parameters.description: The code that will be expanded. If no code is given (default setting) expands the actual source code starting in the given line and ending with the first line ending that is not a line continuation.

parameters.name: contextFile
parameters.description: We simulate that the given code is in this file.

parameters.name: contextLine
parameters.description: We simulate that the given code is in this line of the given file. Defaults to first line.

parameters.name: showDefinedMacros
parameters.description: Whether to display all macros defined at the given line in the given file. Defaults to false since this is a lot of information.

parameters.name: useSynchronizerOutput
parameters.description: Whether to use the output from the content synchronizer, that already does preprocessing and parsing. Defaults to false since we might also want to prevent potential bugs from that stage.

parameters.name: code-scope
parameters.description: The code scope to retrieving the predefined preprocessor macros from the analysis profile. Defaults to the default code scope

get.summary: Verification of the results of the PreprocessorIncludeReparseTrigger
get.description:
This service checks the correctness of one of our preprocessor optimizations on the given project.
For each file where we determined that re-preprocessing the file is not necessary in a commit, the service does preprocess the file again and checks whether the preprocessed tokens really did not change.
Preprocessing in this service is slower than in the normal analysis pipeline. Expect service runtimes of several hours or days.
This service (and the other service) have several configuration options to limit the scope of the correctness checking (e.g., to divide the commit history into chunks and reduce the time for each service call).

This service returns a zip file.
For each commit in which deviations between the stored (i.e., not recomputed in the commit) and recomputed preprocessor results were found, the zip file contains detail information for debugging.
If no deviations are found (i.e., everything is correct), the zip file contains only a "no_problems_found.txt".

This service divides the commits in the project history into batches. Only one batch is analyzed per service call.
You can configure how many commits are contained in a batch and which batch number should be analyzed.
This is meant to be used to break up long service calls that would otherwise be impossible (e.g., would take longer than a VPN login window).

The API requires the user to have View Project permissions on the project.

parameters.name: project
parameters.description: The project ID.

parameters.name: batchSize
parameters.description: The number of commits in a batch.

parameters.name: batchIndex
parameters.description: The index of the batch that should be analyzed. Starts at 0.

parameters.name: fail-fast
parameters.description: Return after first commit in which problems were found.

get.description:
Retrieves a specific count of repository log entries after a start timestamp or before an end timestamp. If both start and end timestamp are missing, the newest log entries are returned. If both a start and end timestamp are provided all log entries between the two are returned. The resulting log entries include all log entries from merged commits into the current branch. The current branch and branch state at a point in time are determined by the commit parameter.
The API requires the user to have View Project permissions on the project.

parameters.name: project
parameters.description: The project ID.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: Start timestamp to return repository log entries after.

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: End timestamp to return repository log entries before.

parameters.name: include-bounds
parameters.description: Whether or not commits for the timestamps from the start and/or end commit are included.

parameters.name: entry-count
parameters.description: The amount of repository logs returned when at least one of the commit parameters is omitted.

parameters.name: t
parameters.description: This parameter can be used to pass a timestamp giving the time (in milliseconds since 1970) for which the data should be provided. This can optionally be prefixed by the name of the branch, followed by a colon. Only log entries from commits merged into the given branch up to the given point will be considered when the start and end timestamps are evaluated.

schema.description: This parameter can be used to pass a timestamp giving the time (in milliseconds since 1970) for which the data should be provided or "HEAD" to refer to the most recent commit. This can optionally be prefixed by the name of the branch, followed by a colon (by default, we use the default branch). You can append a "p1" to resolve to the immediate parent of the most recent commit at the given branch/timestamp. p2 would resolve to the parent of the immediate parent and so on. In merge commits, the parent resolution considers only the first parent. You can append "e" to include the potential external report uploads of the commit. A parameter value that uses all features would be "master:1601637680000p1e"

parameters.name: privacy-aware
parameters.description: Controls whether only repository log entries are returned where the current user was the committer.

parameters.name: commit-types
parameters.description: The commit types to show or hide. If omitted, commits for all types are returned.

parameters.name: commit-attribute
parameters.description: The commit attribute to filter by.

parameters.name: exclude-other-branches
parameters.description: Option whether to filter out commits, that belong to other branches.

parameters.name: users
parameters.description: User names to filter for.

parameters.name: groups
parameters.description: Group names to filter for.

parameters.name: specItemEntriesOnly
parameters.description: Whether we want to filter for spec items or code.

parameters.name: commit-messages
parameters.description: Commit messages to filter for.

get.description:
Returns the comparison information for two Simulink subsystems.
The API requires the user to have View Project permissions on the project.

parameters.name: project
parameters.description: The project ID.

parameters.name: t
parameters.description: This parameter can be used to pass a timestamp giving the time (in milliseconds since 1970) for which the data should be provided. This can optionally be prefixed by the name of the branch, followed by a colon.

schema.description: This parameter can be used to pass a timestamp giving the time (in milliseconds since 1970) for which the data should be provided or "HEAD" to refer to the most recent commit. This can optionally be prefixed by the name of the branch, followed by a colon (by default, we use the default branch). You can append a "p1" to resolve to the immediate parent of the most recent commit at the given branch/timestamp. p2 would resolve to the parent of the immediate parent and so on. In merge commits, the parent resolution considers only the first parent. You can append "e" to include the potential external report uploads of the commit. A parameter value that uses all features would be "master:1601637680000p1e"

parameters.name: left-path
parameters.description: Uniform path of the left model in the comparison

parameters.name: right-path
parameters.description: Uniform path of the right model in the comparison

parameters.name: left-location
parameters.description: Location (ID) of the left subsystem element in the comparison

parameters.name: right-location
parameters.description: Location (ID) of the right subsystem element in the comparison

get.description:
Starts a download of the source code file at the given uniform path.
This service is public API since Teamscale 5.2.
The API requires the user to have View Project permissions on the project.

parameters.name: project
parameters.description: The project ID.

parameters.name: uniformPath
parameters.description: The uniform path of the source code file to be downloaded.

parameters.name: t
parameters.description: The commit for which the source code should be downloaded.

schema.description: This parameter can be used to pass a timestamp giving the time (in milliseconds since 1970) for which the data should be provided or "HEAD" to refer to the most recent commit. This can optionally be prefixed by the name of the branch, followed by a colon (by default, we use the default branch). You can append a "p1" to resolve to the immediate parent of the most recent commit at the given branch/timestamp. p2 would resolve to the parent of the immediate parent and so on. In merge commits, the parent resolution considers only the first parent. You can append "e" to include the potential external report uploads of the commit. A parameter value that uses all features would be "master:1601637680000p1e"

parameters.name: expectFileUnknown
parameters.description: Whether to allow missing files by not logging the error. The eclipse plugin does not know whether files are excluded from Teamscale or not, so we disable the logging of these errors in that case.

parameters.name: representation
parameters.description: The requested representation for the file to be downloaded.

400.description: The file cannot be anonymized as it is a binary file.

401.description: No authentication header was provided or the credentials were invalid.

403.description: The user is authenticated, but does not have the necessary permissions to access the endpoint.

406.description: The file cannot be provided in the requested media type.

You can append a "p1" to resolve to the immediate parent of the most recent commit at the given branch/timestamp. p2 would resolve to the parent of the immediate parent and so on. In merge commits, the parent resolution considers only the first parent. You can append "e" to include the potential external report uploads of the commit. A parameter value that uses all features would be "master:1601637680000p1e"

get.description:
Returns the line coverage data for an element.
The API requires the user to have View Project permissions on the project.

parameters.name: project
parameters.description: The project ID.

parameters.name: all-partitions
parameters.description: If this is true, all available test coverage partitions are considered.

parameters.name: partitions
parameters.description: This parameter may be given multiple times. Specifies the test coverage partitions to consider.

parameters.name: t
parameters.description: This parameter can be used to pass a timestamp giving the time (in milliseconds since 1970) for which the data should be provided. This can optionally be prefixed by the name of the branch, followed by a colon.

schema.description: This parameter can be used to pass a timestamp giving the time (in milliseconds since 1970) for which the data should be provided or "HEAD" to refer to the most recent commit. This can optionally be prefixed by the name of the branch, followed by a colon (by default, we use the default branch). You can append a "p1" to resolve to the immediate parent of the most recent commit at the given branch/timestamp. p2 would resolve to the parent of the immediate parent and so on. In merge commits, the parent resolution considers only the first parent. You can append "e" to include the potential external report uploads of the commit. A parameter value that uses all features would be "master:1601637680000p1e"

parameters.name: include-method-coverage
parameters.description: If true, the coverage will include method-accurate coverage.

parameters.name: only-executed-methods
parameters.description: If this parameter is given, the service disregards code changes and only assesses execution of methods.

parameters.name: uniform-path
parameters.description: Uniform path at which the request should focus on

parameters.name: test-uniform-path
parameters.description: The test uniform path for which coverage should be shown in the treemap. This is an exclusive alternative to test-query i.e. if given, test-query is ignored.

parameters.name: test-query
parameters.description: Test query that determines which coverage should be shown in the treemap. This is an exclusive alternative to test-uniform-path i.e. if test-uniform-path is given, this parameter is ignored.

parameters.name: issue-id
parameters.description: If this parameter is given and is a valid issue ID, information about all methods changed in the context of this issue ID will be returned. In this case, the baseline parameter is ignored (the baseline is determined automatically for the issue instead). If the special value "###no-connector###|[no-issue]" is given, instead all methods that were changed in commits without an issue link between the baseline and end date are returned.

parameters.name: merge-request-mode
parameters.description: If this is true, we will compute TGA information based on the methods changed in the history of commit1 but not in the history of commit2. This simulates a merge from commit1 to commit2.

parameters.name: merge-base-cache-key
parameters.description: Optional key into the cache index for merge base calculation. If this is known it can be used to speed up calculation of the merge base.

parameters.name: merge-request-identifier
parameters.description: Optional merge request identifier to retrieve the merge base info. If this is provided it can be used to directly fetch the merge base info from the merge request delta index before trying to calculate the merge base. This is especially required when creating the TgaBranchMergeRequest for an already merged  merge request.

schema.description: A merge request identifier, which consists of a connector ID and a merge request number seperated by a slash

parameters.name: auto-select-branch
parameters.description: Will auto-select the issue branch if set to true. Otherwise, if given, the branchName will be used. As fallback the default branch will be used.

parameters.name: branch-name
parameters.description: If this is given, all issues are shown on this branch. Otherwise, the branch is auto-determined for each issue separately.

parameters.name: include-child-issues
parameters.description: Will include changes introduced by child issues.

parameters.name: execution-only
parameters.description: If this parameter is given, the service disregards code changes and only assesses execution/coverage of methods. The result will not contain information about test gaps, but only about which methods were executed.

parameters.name: churn
parameters.description: Whether we want to view only the churn. This means coverage is ignored for the assessment and methods are only classified as unchanged, added or modified.

parameters.name: exclude-unchanged-methods
parameters.description: Parameter determining if only changed methods should be returned

400.description: Could not determine common merge base.

401.description: No authentication header was provided or the credentials were invalid.

403.description: The user is authenticated, but does not have the necessary permissions to access the endpoint.

409.description: Found multiple issues for the provided id.

In merge commits, the parent resolution considers only the first parent. You can append "e" to include the potential external report uploads of the commit. A parameter value that uses all features would be "master:1601637680000p1e"

parameters.description: The sort order

parameters.description: This parameter can be used to pass a timestamp giving the time (in milliseconds since 1970) for which the data should be provided. This can optionally be prefixed by the name of the branch, followed by a colon.

schema.description: This parameter can be used to pass a timestamp giving the time (in milliseconds since 1970) for which the data should be provided or "HEAD" to refer to the most recent commit. This can optionally be prefixed by the name of the branch, followed by a colon (by default, we use the default branch). You can append a "p1" to resolve to the immediate parent of the most recent commit at the given branch/timestamp. p2 would resolve to the parent of the immediate parent and so on. In merge commits, the parent resolution considers only the first parent. You can append "e" to include the potential external report uploads of the commit. A parameter value that uses all features would be "master:1601637680000p1e"

parameters.name: on-partition-level
parameters.description: If true, one result entry is returned per uniform path and partition

parameters.name: only-with-coverage
parameters.description: If true only tests with testwise coverage will be returned

get.summary: Get the set of tests that are related to the given code delta. The code delta is defined by a baseline and an issue id.
get.description:
Tests are selected based on information retrieval techniques. The scoring operator to use can be configured by a corresponding parameter. If an issue id is provided, all code modified or added in context of the issue is considered. If both a baseline and an issue id are provided, the union of the deltas is considered.
The API requires the user to have Retrieve Test Suggestions permissions on the project.

parameters.name: project
parameters.description: The project ID.

parameters.name: baseline
parameters.description: The baseline commit (exclusive). This parameter can be used to pass a timestamp giving the time (in milliseconds since 1970) for which the data should be provided or "HEAD" to refer to the most recent commit. This can optionally be prefixed by the name of the branch, followed by a colon (by default, we use the default branch). You can append a "p1" to resolve to the immediate parent of the most recent commit at the given branch/timestamp. p2 would resolve to the parent of the immediate parent and so on. In merge commits, the parent resolution considers only the first parent. You can append "e" to include the potential external report uploads of the commit. A parameter value that uses all features would be "master:1601637680000p1e"

schema.description: This parameter can be used to pass a timestamp giving the time (in milliseconds since 1970) for which the data should be provided or "HEAD" to refer to the most recent commit. This can optionally be prefixed by the name of the branch, followed by a colon (by default, we use the default branch). You can append a "p1" to resolve to the immediate parent of the most recent commit at the given branch/timestamp. p2 would resolve to the parent of the immediate parent and so on. In merge commits, the parent resolution considers only the first parent. You can append "e" to include the potential external report uploads of the commit. A parameter value that uses all features would be "master:1601637680000p1e"

parameters.name: issue-id
parameters.description: An issue id used do determine the changed code, that is, all commits and their code linked to the issue.Format: <issue connector id>|<issue id> (that is, separated with a pipe symbol).

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: The end commit (inclusive). This parameter can be used to pass a timestamp giving the time (in milliseconds since 1970) for which the data should be provided or "HEAD" to refer to the most recent commit. This can optionally be prefixed by the name of the branch, followed by a colon (by default, we use the default branch). You can append a "p1" to resolve to the immediate parent of the most recent commit at the given branch/timestamp. p2 would resolve to the parent of the immediate parent and so on. In merge commits, the parent resolution considers only the first parent. You can append "e" to include the potential external report uploads of the commit. A parameter value that uses all features would be "master:1601637680000p1e"

schema.description: This parameter can be used to pass a timestamp giving the time (in milliseconds since 1970) for which the data should be provided or "HEAD" to refer to the most recent commit. This can optionally be prefixed by the name of the branch, followed by a colon (by default, we use the default branch). You can append a "p1" to resolve to the immediate parent of the most recent commit at the given branch/timestamp. p2 would resolve to the parent of the immediate parent and so on. In merge commits, the parent resolution considers only the first parent. You can append "e" to include the potential external report uploads of the commit. A parameter value that uses all features would be "master:1601637680000p1e"

parameters.name: repository
parameters.description: When using the revision parameter, this parameter allows to pass a repository name which is used to identify the correct commit in situations where the same revision exists in multiple repositories.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Select only from tests specified by the given query

parameters.name: ensure-processed
parameters.description: If set to true the request will fail with '412 PRECONDITION FAILED' if the exact given end commit has not been processed yet and therefore data may not be up-to-date.

parameters.name: scoring-operator
parameters.description: Strategy to use for computing the score of a test for a given change. Default: BM25

parameters.name: provide-top-contributing-terms
parameters.description: With each scored test, also provide the top k terms that contributed to the final score.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Limit the number of returned tests to the given number. Default: -1, meaning that all are returned.

400.description: One of the given options is invalid.

401.description: No authentication header was provided or the credentials were invalid.

403.description: The user is authenticated, but does not have the necessary permissions to access the endpoint.

412.description: The given commit has not been processed yet.

get.description:
Returns a plain text GraphViz DOT description of the trigger graph
The API requires the user to have View Project permissions on the project.

parameters.name: project
parameters.description: The project ID.

parameters.name: include-indexes
parameters.description: Whether read/written indexes are included as nodes in the graph.

parameters.name: slice-nodes
parameters.description: Names of nodes in the graph that should be used to start slicing. This can be trigger names or index names. To slice by index, use the index name, not the index-class name (e.g., check-phase-dependencies, not CheckPhaseResultDependencyIndex). All nodes that are not forward/backward reachable from these nodes are not displayed.Nodes are matched exact, so provide the exact names of triggers/indexes that you want to be included.If the list is empty, the complete graph is returned.

parameters.name: forward-slice
parameters.description: If true (forward slicing), only nodes influenced by the slice nodes are returned (i.e., nodes that are executed "after" the slice nodes). If false (backward slicing), we return only nodes that influence one of the slice nodes.

get.summary: Get visualization of the trigger graph
get.description:
Retrieves a visualization of the trigger graph. The service returns the graph in dot format (graph viz). To render a picture from that, I recommend online tools (e.g., https://stamm-wilbrandt.de/GraphvizFiddle/).

If you specified a slice node, but get an empty graph, we could probably not match the given string to a node. Download a complete graph and copy the name from there.
The API requires the user to have View Project permissions on the project.

parameters.name: project
parameters.description: The project ID.

parameters.name: include-indexes
parameters.description: If this parameter is 'true', then the read/written indexes are included in the graph.

parameters.name: slice-nodes
parameters.description: Names of nodes in the graph that should be used to start slicing. This can be trigger names or index names. To slice by index, use the index name, not the index-class name (e.g., check-phase-dependencies, not CheckPhaseResultDependencyIndex). All nodes that are not forward/backward reachable from these nodes are not displayed.Nodes are matched exact, so provide the exact names of triggers/indexes that you want to be included.If the list is empty, the complete graph is returned.

parameters.name: forward-slice
parameters.description: If true (forward slicing), only nodes influenced by the slice nodes are returned (i.e., nodes that are executed "after" the slice nodes). If false (backward slicing), we return only nodes that influence one of the slice nodes.

The API requires the user to have View Project permissions on the project.

parameters.name: project
parameters.description: The project ID.

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: The end commit (inclusive). This parameter can be used to pass a timestamp giving the time (in milliseconds since 1970) for which the data should be provided or "HEAD" to refer to the most recent commit. This can optionally be prefixed by the name of the branch, followed by a colon (by default, we use the default branch). You can append a "p1" to resolve to the immediate parent of the most recent commit at the given branch/timestamp. p2 would resolve to the parent of the immediate parent and so on. In merge commits, the parent resolution considers only the first parent. You can append "e" to include the potential external report uploads of the commit. A parameter value that uses all features would be "master:1601637680000p1e"

get.description:
Returns the number of unique users between the day of the given start timestamp (inclusive) and the day of the given end timestamp (inclusive). There are two ways in which you can call this service: 1) Provide only the baseline timestamp to get the number of unique users on the day of the timestamp. 2) Provide a baseline and end timestamp to get the number of unique users on every day from start of the day of the baseline to the end of the day of the end timestamp
The API requires the user to have View User Statistics permissions.

parameters.name: project-ids
parameters.description: List of all ids of the projects.

parameters.name: baseline
parameters.description: The baseline commit (inclusive). This parameter can be used to pass a timestamp giving the time (in milliseconds since 1970) for which the data should be provided or "HEAD" to refer to the most recent commit. This can optionally be prefixed by the name of the branch, followed by a colon (by default, we use the default branch). You can append a "p1" to resolve to the immediate parent of the most recent commit at the given branch/timestamp. p2 would resolve to the parent of the immediate parent and so on. In merge commits, the parent resolution considers only the first parent. You can append "e" to include the potential external report uploads of the commit. A parameter value that uses all features would be "master:1601637680000p1e"

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: The end commit (inclusive). This parameter can be used to pass a timestamp giving the time (in milliseconds since 1970) for which the data should be provided or "HEAD" to refer to the most recent commit. This can optionally be prefixed by the name of the branch, followed by a colon (by default, we use the default branch). You can append a "p1" to resolve to the immediate parent of the most recent commit at the given branch/timestamp. p2 would resolve to the parent of the immediate parent and so on. In merge commits, the parent resolution considers only the first parent. You can append "e" to include the potential external report uploads of the commit. A parameter value that uses all features would be "master:1601637680000p1e" If this is not given, only one trend entry will be returned, which is for the day on which the given baseline timestamp lies.

parameters.name: exclude-group
parameters.description: Users in these groups are ignored in the user count. Group names can contain spaces.

401.description: No authentication header was provided or the credentials were invalid.

403.description: The user is authenticated, but does not have the necessary permissions to access the endpoint.

parameters.name: project-ids
parameters.description: List of all ids of the projects.

parameters.name: baseline
parameters.description: The baseline commit (inclusive). This parameter can be used to pass a timestamp giving the time (in milliseconds since 1970) for which the data should be provided or "HEAD" to refer to the most recent commit. This can optionally be prefixed by the name of the branch, followed by a colon (by default, we use the default branch). You can append a "p1" to resolve to the immediate parent of the most recent commit at the given branch/timestamp. p2 would resolve to the parent of the immediate parent and so on. In merge commits, the parent resolution considers only the first parent. You can append "e" to include the potential external report uploads of the commit. A parameter value that uses all features would be "master:1601637680000p1e"

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: The end commit (inclusive). This parameter can be used to pass a timestamp giving the time (in milliseconds since 1970) for which the data should be provided or "HEAD" to refer to the most recent commit. This can optionally be prefixed by the name of the branch, followed by a colon (by default, we use the default branch). You can append a "p1" to resolve to the immediate parent of the most recent commit at the given branch/timestamp. p2 would resolve to the parent of the immediate parent and so on. In merge commits, the parent resolution considers only the first parent. You can append "e" to include the potential external report uploads of the commit. A parameter value that uses all features would be "master:1601637680000p1e" If this is not given, only one trend entry will be returned, which is for the day on which the given baseline timestamp lies.

parameters.name: exclude-group
parameters.description: Users in these groups are ignored in the user count. Group names can contain spaces.

colorBlindMode.description: Whether color-blind specific colors should be used

FindingsSeverityColorOption.description: Colors every node according to the worst severity of their assigned findings, 'green' if a node has no findings.

IColorOption.description: Describes how the treemap nodes should be colored.

IMetricOption.description: Describes the respective metric and appropriate parameters, that should be shown within the treemap

colorMaxReferencesValue.description: The minimum number of issue references a node must have to be colored in `maxReferencesColor`. Use `-1` to automatically calculate the maximum number within the treemap.

colorMinReferencesValue.description: The maximum number of issue references a node can have to be colored in `minReferencesColor`. Use `-1` to automatically calculate the minimum number within the treemap.

MatchedIssuesColorOption.description: Colors every method/node depending on their number of matched issues with a gradient from `minReferencesColor` to `maxReferencesColor`.

statisticsAverage.description: The average statistics calculation for all methods (even the ones not referenced by enough issues, if not excluded)

statisticsAverageForIssueReferenced.description: The average statistics calculation for methods referenced by an issue. `null` if no issues are loaded.

colorMaxFindingsValue.description: The minimum number of findings a node must have to be colored in `maxFindingsColor`. Use `-1` to automatically calculate the maximum number within the treemap.

colorMinFindingsValue.description: The maximum number of findings a node can have to be colored in `minFindingsColor`. Use `-1` to automatically calculate the minimum number within the treemap.

NumberOfFindingsColorOption.description: Colors every node depending on their number of findings with a gradient from `minFindingsColor` to `maxFindingsColor`
